decks.notes: The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain a fast-paced and aggressive playstyle, utilizing cheap spells and prowess creatures to chip away at the opponent's life total while disrupting their game plan with bounce effects and burn spells. The deck aims to apply early pressure through efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also having card draw and spell recursion to maintain gas in the later game. The deck is designed to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize and establish control.

This UW Control deck focuses on using a variety of spells and enchantments to disrupt opponents while generating card advantage and controlling the board. With cards like Beza, the Bounding Spring and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, the deck can bounce threats and counter spells, while Consult the Star Charts and March of Otherworldly Light provide card draw and removal. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and ultimately control the game through careful resource management and efficient spell usage.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of cheap spells and talented creatures to apply constant pressure on the opponent. By leveraging efficient removal spells and powerful tempo plays, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory. Utilizing a mix of powerful creatures like Gran-Gran and Vivi Ornitier, alongside versatile spells like Divide by Zero and Combustion Technique, the deck is designed to maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy. With a focus on aggression and adaptability, this deck aims to establish dominance and control the pace of the game.

This deck focuses on a fast and aggressive play style, utilizing cheap spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The key idea is to maintain pressure on the opponent and control the board with efficient removal spells while also keeping card advantage through card draw effects. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of direct damage spells and creatures with prowess ability, making it difficult for the opponent to stabilize and turn the game in their favor.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently get Arclight Phoenix into play by utilizing cards like Opt, Sleight of Hand, and Consider to draw through the deck and fill the graveyard. Fiery Impulse and Lightning Axe provide removal for any threats, while Treasure Cruise helps to refill the hand. Picklock Prankster and Artist's Talent can help generate additional value. The deck also incorporates Prismari Command and Prismari Charm for flexibility in dealing with different situations. Otawara, Soaring City and Into the Flood Maw provide additional support for the overall game plan.

The key concept of the deck is to apply aggressive pressure on the opponent using a combination of low-cost creatures and disruptive spells. The deck utilizes Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker to quickly build a board presence, while also having access to disruption through cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. Mutavault and various other lands provide additional versatility and power to the deck. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient creature-based strategy while maintaining control over the game with its disruptive elements.

The main focus of this deck is to consistently cast spells to fuel the Arclight Phoenix ability and bring it back from the graveyard. By utilizing low-cost spells and card draw effects, the deck aims to rapidly fill the graveyard and bring back Arclight Phoenix multiple times in a game. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Traumatic Critique and Picklock Prankster to control the opponent's board while maintaining pressure with the flying Phoenix. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, allowing the deck to efficiently execute its game plan.

This deck focuses on assembling the powerful combo of Lotus Field with Hidden Strings and untap effects to generate large amounts of mana quickly. By using cards like Arboreal Grazer, Sylvan Scrying, and Vizier of Tumbling Sands to find and untap Lotus Field, the deck can cast huge spells like Emergent Ultimatum and Omniscience to overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Lier, Disciple of the Drowned provide additional utility and control options, making it a versatile and powerful combo deck in the Pioneer format.

The deck focuses on stalling and controlling the game with counterspells, removal, and card draw until it can unleash powerful threats like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Ultima. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up resources and card advantage to ultimately overwhelm them with a combination of control tools and powerful finishers. The deck utilizes a mix of instant-speed interaction and proactive threats to keep the opponent off balance and dictate the pace of the game.

The focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively play Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker to deal damage to the opponent while controlling the board with Floodpit Drowner and removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat. Multiversal Passage and Gloomlake Verge help to fix mana for the deck, while cards like Thoughtseize and Enduring Curiosity provide disruption and card advantage. Kaito, Bane of Nightmares acts as a finisher, supported by Mockingbird and Cecil, Dark Knight. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of evasive threats and disruption.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to slow down the opponent's game plan, while deploying efficient threats like Skyclave Apparition and Collected Company to apply pressure, the deck aims to quickly finish off the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of powerful planeswalkers like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and versatile lands like Boseiju, Who Endures adds additional ways to close out the game and ensure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively rush down the opponent with cheap and efficient red creatures and burn spells. By applying early pressure and utilizing cards like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage to remove opponent's threats, the deck aims to maintain board control and close out the game quickly. The inclusion of cards like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional reach and damage potential, while cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear help to increase the deck's overall tempo and aggression.

The focus of this deck is to create a powerful combo using Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate value and overwhelm the opponent. This is supported by cards that provide disruption, card advantage, and mana fixing to ensure the combo can be assembled efficiently. The deck also includes creatures and spells that can further support the combo strategy by providing additional threats or utility. Overall, the key concept is to efficiently assemble and protect the Greasefang Parhelion combo to secure victory.

This deck focuses on playing aggressively with a mix of tempo and burn spells. It aims to control the board early on with cheap spells like Spell Pierce and Divide by Zero, while also dealing damage with creatures like Stormchaser's Talent and Gran-Gran. The deck also incorporates card draw and card advantage through spells like Consider and Accumulate Wisdom. With a versatile mix of burn, control, and creatures, the deck aims to quickly defeat opponents before they can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm opponents. It also includes support cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the game and protect the combo pieces. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the color requirements of the deck. Overall, the key concept is to efficiently assemble the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Parhelion II. The deck also uses control cards like Fatal Push, Duress, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The land base is designed to support the combo pieces and provide a stable mana base. Overall, the deck aims to quickly set up the combo and protect it while controlling the opponent's board.
